title: Partition String into Independent Slices
description:
### Objective
Given a string s, partition it into as many parts as possible so that each letter appears in at most one part, and return a list of the sizes of these parts.

### Function Contract
Implement `partition_labels(s: str)` in Python.
Do not change input/output semantics expected by the tests.

### Input and Output
- Input `s`: type `string` (example: `"ababcbacadefegdehijhklij"`).
- Return type: `array`.

### Requirements
- Return the exact expected output for every test case.
- Handle edge cases (empty input, duplicates, boundary values) without raising exceptions.
- Target complexity: time `O(n)`, space `O(1)`.

### Constraints
- 1 ≤ |s| ≤ 500
explanation:
Intuition
Greedy based on last occurrence. Track the rightmost index for each character. Scan once, extending the current partition's right boundary to the furthest last occurrence seen so far. When index meets boundary, close a partition.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Record last index of each char
2. Main Algorithm: Scan s, update right=max(right, last[s[i]]); when i==right, append size and start new segment
3. Return Result: Return sizes

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n)
- Space Complexity: O(1) since alphabet is fixed

Key Insights
- Right boundary only moves forward
- Each index belongs to exactly one partition

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting to include the last segment
- Using first instead of last occurrence breaks correctness
hints: Map each char to its last index
Expand partition right to the furthest last
